setup: Copy the fixture vault to a scratch dir outside the repo, `git init -b main` it, commit everything, and copy skills/next-card and skills/ship-card into <scratch>/.agents/skills/. Run the eval agent with cwd=<scratch>, no network, and `gh` unavailable. See skills/evals/README.md.

evals.name: ready-card-happy-path
evals.expected_output: Pre-flights, reads the card, plans against its Acceptance, finds no blockers (JB-101 is Shipped) and continues without waiting. Branches as job/JB-102-hello-page, sets status Doing (frontmatter only, updated bumped), creates docs/hello.md per the Plan without touching docs/outline.md, closes the card with --finish (status Shipped, boxes ticked) folded into the final Conventional Commit, and — gh being unavailable — hands back the branch with a PR body draft that maps Acceptance to proof and includes a How to test section.

assertions.text: Branch is job/JB-102-hello-page (type label lower-cased, id casing kept) — unless already on a harness branch
Writes a short plan referencing the card's Acceptance before branching, and does not stall waiting for approval when nothing is wrong
Sets status to Doing via a frontmatter-only edit with updated bumped (ISO 8601 UTC)
Creates docs/hello.md per the Plan and does not modify docs/outline.md (Out of scope is binding)
Closes the card via the script's --finish (status Shipped, Acceptance boxes ticked) folded into the final commit, not a trailing one
Commit message and PR title are Conventional Commits
With gh unavailable, falls back gracefully (compare URL or clear note) and the PR body draft maps each Acceptance bullet to its proof, with a How to test section a stranger can follow
Raises no in-flight touches-overlap doubt — JB-102's surface (prose) collides with nothing in flight, so pre-flight adds no overlap noise

evals.name: blocked-card-stops
evals.prompt: Ship JB-103 and open a PR for it.
evals.expected_output: Pre-flights, sees status Blocked and the unmet dependency JB-104 (Queued, not Shipped), and stops with a concrete question — work JB-104 first, or proceed anyway? No branch, no code, no card edits.

assertions.text: Creates no branch and writes no implementation code
Names the blockers: Blocked status and/or unmet dependency JB-104
Ends with a concrete, answerable question proposing options, not a silent stop or silent proceed

evals.name: container-card-in-scope
evals.expected_output: Does NOT refuse the container. Pre-flight lists the children: JB-101 already Shipped, JB-102 unfinished and therefore in scope. Plans to deliver EP-100 by shipping JB-102's work, then proceeds (or asks only a genuinely necessary scope question).

assertions.text: Does not treat the container as an error or redirect away from it
Identifies JB-102 as the unfinished child whose work is in scope, and JB-101 as already done
Any pause carries a concrete question; otherwise it proceeds to plan/build the child's work under the epic

evals.name: harness-branch-stay
evals.expected_output: Setup places the agent on a pre-existing branch it didn't create (e.g. copilot/do-jb-102). The agent stays on it — no switch to main, no new job/JB-102-… branch — and otherwise ships normally.

assertions.text: Performs no git switch / checkout to main or to a new branch; all commits land on the pre-existing branch
Does not treat the branch name as a problem

evals.name: not-a-vault-refusal
evals.expected_output: Run from a directory with no .mos/config.json at or above it: states this isn't a mos vault and stops. No branch, no guessing at cards.

assertions.text: Refuses, naming the missing .mos/config.json
Creates nothing and invents nothing

evals.name: inflight-overlap-doubt
evals.expected_output: Pre-flights a ready, well-formed card. The only soft spot is a touches overlap: EP-106 declares `data`, and JB-103 — in flight in the Doing column — also declares `data`, so the two would edit the same surface. The agent surfaces this as a doubt before building (name JB-103 and the shared `data` area), and treats it as a coordinate/sequence/proceed decision rather than an automatic refusal. It does NOT silently ignore the overlap, and does NOT treat it as a hard blocker.

assertions.text: Surfaces the in-flight overlap before building: names JB-103 and the shared area `data`
Treats it as a doubt to weigh (sequence, coordinate, or proceed deliberately) — not an automatic stop and not silently ignored
Does not invent a dependency or refuse outright; EP-106 is otherwise ready (no unmet deps, has Acceptance)
